funding_carry.name: Funding-rate carry (delta-neutral)
funding_carry.economic_rationale: Spot long + perp short when funding is high: the perp shorts PAY me the funding. Real recurring cash flow, not a price prediction.

tests.note: pre-registered single config; pooled across 4 majors
pre-registered single config; pooled across 4 majors
Stage4 gauntlet 5/6 -> FAIL param_plateau (50% configs positive, param-sensitive). NOT deployable.
Continuous carry OVER-TRADES: net APR -4.14%/yr, 2/6. Selective(threshold) was better but fragile. Carry real but too small to robustly harvest at retail unleveraged w/ pessimistic costs.

basis_conv.name: Basis convergence
basis_conv.economic_rationale: Perp/futures price converges to spot at/over time; capture the spread as it closes.

liq_meanrev.name: Liquidation-cascade mean reversion
liq_meanrev.economic_rationale: Forced liquidations overshoot price; I provide liquidity into the cascade and fade it.

tests.note: REJECTED pre-backtest. Premise is directionally TRUE (78.4% of forced fills are worse than mark -> overshoot is REAL) but the discount is UNCAPTURABLE: pooled notional-weighted median +1.34bps vs 5bps bar. Fails at EVERY cost level incl. theoretical best case (2bps maker/maker -> net -0.66bps). ROOT CAUSE: discount is inversely proportional to size (Spearman -0.36). The <$10 bucket shows +74.5bps but is 0.20% of notional (avg fill $3.71); the $1k-10k bucket is 52.8% of notional and shows +0.70bps. Kraken EPP shreds liqs into 10% child orders absorbed near mark by resting MMs; only scraps that walk the book get a real discount, and scraps are not a business. NEW TRAP FOUND: the UNWEIGHTED median (+74.87bps on DOGE, +1.80 pooled) looks like a large edge. Notional-weighting collapses it to +2.60/+1.34. Any analysis of forced-flow fills without notional weighting will manufacture a fake edge.

liq_meanrev.notes: PRE-REGISTERED 2026-07-17, BEFORE seeing any data (Kraken PF_* liquidation tape). Decisive test is NOT a backtest: each execution carries markPrice at that instant, so dislocation = (fill-mark)/mark is directly observable. Sign folded by direction so adverse_bps>0 == filled WORSE than mark == a discount available to the other side. || FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION: if the premise ("forced liquidations overshoot") is true, median adverse_bps across liquidations must be POSITIVE and exceed round-trip maker cost (~4-5bps: Kraken maker ~2bps/side + spread). || PRE-SET BAR: median adverse_bps <= 0  => premise FALSE, reject outright, no backtest. 0 < median <= 5bps => real but uncapturable at retail cost, reject. median > 5bps => proceed to Stage 4 gauntlet. || NOTE the counter-evidence already in hand: the single record inspected pre-pull filled a forced Sell ~+0.6bps ABOVE mark (limitFilled=false), i.e. adverse_bps NEGATIVE. n=1, but it points against the premise. Recording this now so a later negative result cannot be reframed as expected.

vol_momo_alts.name: Vol-filtered momentum on mid-cap alts
vol_momo_alts.economic_rationale: Institutions avoid small caps for capacity reasons, leaving momentum less arbitraged; trade it only in calm-vol regimes to cut whipsaw.

tsmom.name: Time-series momentum (vol-scaled)
tsmom.economic_rationale: Trend persistence: past positive return predicts near-term positive (Liu-Tsyvinski up to 8wk). Prediction, not cash flow -- must survive brutal costs.

tests.note: Stage4 gauntlet 3/6 -> FAIL pbo(0.76)+dsr(0)+falsification(p=0.15). No real edge. Rejected.

tsmom.notes: Research: TSMOM>CSMOM in crypto but gross often insignificant net of costs.

xexch_arb.name: Cross-exchange funding arbitrage
xexch_arb.economic_rationale: Same perp, different exchanges, different funding (fragmented liquidity). Long the exchange paying longs + short the one paying shorts => market-neutral, collect the funding SPREAD. A structural edge, not a prediction.

tests.note: REJECTED. Mechanism REAL (fragmented liquidity does produce persistent funding differences) but UNCAPTURABLE: the spread mean-reverts in HOURS while the one-time round-trip cost needs DAYS to amortise. SURVIVAL: 42 episodes above 6% across 4 coins; 0 lasted past the 163h breakeven. Longest single episode = 13.8h (ETH) vs 163h needed — short by 12x. Medians: BTC 0.84h, ETH 7.24h, SOL 3.38h, XRP 0.84h. AUTOCORR of spread: 0.86 @10min, 0.25 @1h, NEGATIVE (-0.17) @8h => it does not decay, it FLIPS. Leg-pair flipped 9/7/15/28 times in 260 polls (each flip = a full round-trip cost). arb_calc net_at(30d) = +13.4% APR was fiction: it assumed a 30-day hold on a spread whose longest observed life is 13.8 HOURS. It also omitted the availability multiplier (SOL 70% => 6.7% became ~4.7%). Both now fixed. SAMPLE CAVEAT: 51h, ~6 independent OKX settlements. Small. But 42 episodes with 0 survivors and a 12x shortfall is not a marginal call. MEASUREMENT BUG ALSO FOUND: spread_pct subtracted Kraken (locked forward hourly rate) from OKX (still-updating 8h estimate) — two different objects. And Bybit was present in only 4/1036 rows (geo-blocked on GitHub runners), so the "cross-exchange" monitor was Kraken-vs-OKX only. KEY LESSON: we planned to wait 2-4 WEEKS for this answer. 2 DAYS sufficed — because the event we were waiting to observe happens 42 times in 2 days and never lasts. Short-lived phenomena need short windows, not long ones.

xexch_arb.notes: DISCIPLINE GAP: this ran as our ACTIVE BET from 2026-07-15 without ever being registered. Registered retroactively at the moment of its rejection. Every idea gets registered BEFORE it runs — this one did not, and that is a process failure worth recording, not hiding.

delist_event.name: Perp delisting forced-settlement event
delist_event.economic_rationale: Bybit force-closes ALL open positions in a delisted perp at a 30-min index average, on ~2 days notice. Holders cannot decline at any price, and their arb counterparties must unwind the hedged leg too. Market makers rationally withdraw from a contract with a known death date. So: price-insensitive forced exits into deliberately thinning liquidity. This is the purest FORCED-SETTLE in FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md (S1.3) and the first PERCENT-scale candidate we have tested — every prior rejection died because the edge was bps-scale and retail cost is ~5bps.

params.test: event study, BTC-adjusted, own-control (pre vs post drift)
params.sample: 193 single USDT-perp delistings; 186 usable, 6 relisted, 1 no-kline

tests.note: REJECTED — and the sign came out OPPOSITE to the prediction. Predicted: announcement -> negative abnormal return. Observed: the decline DECELERATES. pre-announcement abnormal drift median -4.31%, post -1.72%, so INCREMENTAL = +2.42% (volume-weighted +11.80%) vs a -0.50% bar. THE CONFOUND WAS THE WHOLE ANSWER: raw post-announcement return is -1.72%, which without the pre-drift control reads as a clean short signal. It is not. The coin was already falling faster BEFORE the news. Shorting on announcement yields +1.72% median; shorting the SAME coin 48h EARLIER yields +4.31%. The announcement makes you WORSE off by -2.59% — it is the worst moment to short, not the best. MECHANISM (monotonic, n=186, quartiles by pre-drift): worst-crash bucket pre -21.44% -> post -6.83% (incremental +17.30%); flat/up bucket pre +3.32% -> post +0.38% (incremental -3.15%). Textbook mean-reversion after a crash. REVERSE CAUSALITY: Bybit DDM auto-delists when last price < 20x tickSize, so the announcement is TRIGGERED BY the crash — a lagging indicator that arrives near the bottom. 45% of delisted coins were already down >5% vs BTC before the news. STRUCTURAL ERROR IN THE HYPOTHESIS ITSELF (the real lesson): I called delisting "price-insensitive forced selling". It is not selling at all. A perp is zero-net-supply — EVERY long is matched by a short. Delisting cash-settles BOTH sides at a 30-min index average. Cash settlement touches no order book, and the two sides net to zero by construction. There is no forced flow: there is forced SETTLEMENT, which is the OPPOSITE — a guarantee you need not trade at all. The only real flow is people electing to exit before settlement, which is two-sided and self-cancelling — the same category as the funding "dodge" (FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md 4.2). FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md 1.3 calls this "the purest forced flow that exists". That is WRONG and is now corrected. NOTE the weighting went the other way this time: volume-weighted incremental (+11.80%) is MORE positive than count-weighted (+2.42%) — in the larger coins the bounce is stronger still. Weighting is not always a haircut; it is just the truth.

delist_event.notes: PRE-REGISTERED 2026-07-17 BEFORE computing any return. Data recon only: 448 announcements in Bybit free API; klines for CLOSED symbols ARE retrievable with an explicit start/end (default returns latest bars = empty, which looks like no data but is not). || PREDICTION: announcement -> negative abnormal return, concentrated in the ~2 days between publishTime and the stated delisting time. || THE CONFOUND THAT DECIDES THIS (must be handled, not mentioned): SELECTION BIAS. Bybit delists coins that are ALREADY DYING - its own DDM rule delists when last price < 20x tickSize. So a post-announcement fall may be the coin simply continuing to die, and would have happened with no announcement at all. Therefore the measured quantity is NOT raw return. It is: (a) abnormal return vs BTC over the same window, AND (b) that abnormal return MINUS the same coins own pre-announcement abnormal drift. If the post-announcement drift merely continues the pre-announcement drift, the announcement carries NO information and the hypothesis is dead regardless of how negative the raw return looks. || PRE-SET BAR (fixed now, cannot be moved later): the incremental abnormal return (post-drift minus pre-drift) must be more negative than -50bps ROUND TRIP. 50bps is the honest cost for a DYING MICROCAP perp - 5.5bps taker each way is the fee, but the spread on a contract with a death date is the real cost, and it widens exactly when we would trade. If |incremental| <= 50bps => reject, uncapturable, same as the last three. || MUST ALSO SURVIVE (my own red flag from liq_meanrev, applied to myself): NOTIONAL WEIGHTING. If the effect exists only in coins with negligible traded volume, it is not a business - it is the .71-fill trap in a new costume. Report count-weighted AND volume-weighted; the volume-weighted number decides. || PRIOR: FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md S4.1 records that a predictable price response to PERP delisting is UNVERIFIED folklore - the literature covers spot/regulatory events, not perp forced settlement. The mechanism is certain; the price response is not. Expect rejection.

funding_escalation.name: Funding-interval escalation as a carry entry signal
funding_escalation.economic_rationale: OKX escalates settlement frequency 8h->4h->2h->1h when the funding rate hits its cap AT SETTLEMENT (published rule, effective 2026-04-14). A binding cap means funding has STOPPED clearing the basis: the paying side is subsidised, so the premium has no reason to close. Escalation un-clamps it. Because the 8/N divisor lands BEFORE the clamp, going 8h->1h can restore up to 8x the daily carry (at premium P=1%: 1.125%/day capped vs 3.00%/day uncapped). WHO PAYS ME: leveraged traders so desperate for exposure that funding is pinned at the exchange maximum. I take the other side and collect a rate the exchange itself just raised.

tests.note: PASSES the pre-registered 40bps bar — the FIRST idea in this project to do so. Recording it as tested, NOT passed: napkin PASS earns a gauntlet run, not a deployment. DATA UNLOCK: I claimed this needed WEEKS of live snapshotting. Wrong — the gap between consecutive fundingTime stamps IS the interval, so escalation history reconstructs retroactively. Same mistake shape as the Kraken liquidation endpoint. RESULT: 10 episodes / 507 symbols / 16d. Median capture 322.9bps vs 40bps bar, 90% of episodes clear it. TIMING CHECK PASSES: only 12.8% of the money sits in the trigger settlement, so the bulk is still available AFTER escalation becomes observable — this is NOT a lagging indicator like delist_event. Median duration 26h. THEN THE FILTERS BIT. 9/10 episodes have NEGATIVE funding => to collect you go LONG perp => the delta-neutral hedge is SHORT SPOT => you must BORROW the coin. Only 6/10 have spot at all; only 4/10 are also borrowable. Real universe = 4 episodes in 16 days across the entire 507-symbol book, i.e. ~1 per 4 days. CAPACITY IS THE BINDING CONSTRAINT: OKX basic borrow quotas are tiny in dollars — LRC $2,174, BREV $1,834, BLUR $3,108, ME $7,622 (vs BTC $11.2M, which confirms the quota field is coin units). Honest per-episode P&L after 48bps execution + borrow interest: LRC +$105.53, BLUR +$235.08, ME +$116.70, BREV +$0.26. Total $457.57 over 16 days => ~$10,438/yr IF every episode is caught with full quota. Napkin verdict with honest inputs (edge 394bps, cost 67bps, capacity $2,641, 91/yr): PASS, +327bps net per round trip. || THE QUESTION THAT DECIDES IT, AND IT IS NOT ANSWERABLE FROM HISTORY: why has this not been arbed away? Best hypothesis, and it is uncomfortable: funding is pinned at the cap PRECISELY BECAUSE the borrow is unavailable. If borrow were free, someone would short spot / long perp and funding would normalise immediately. If that is right, the $2,174 quota is not merely a capacity ceiling — it is the REASON the opportunity exists, and during an episode every would-be arbitrageur is competing for the same exhausted pool. We may not be able to borrow at all when it matters. || OTHER REASONS TO DISTRUST THIS: n=4 is an anecdote, not a strategy; 1 of the 4 (BREV) netted $0.26, i.e. nothing; the 48bps execution assumption presumes maker fills on all four legs during a stress event in a microcap; the borrow rate used is the BASIC rate and OKX raises it dynamically under demand — exactly when we would borrow. || NEXT: do NOT deploy. Live-test borrow availability during an episode (the snapshotter already flags escalation in real time), and accumulate n.

params.test: live follow-up, 4 days of snapshotter data

tests.note: LIVE FOLLOW-UP WEAKENS THE CASE ON THREE COUNTS. Still tested, not rejected — but each of these has to be answered before it goes near the gauntlet. || 1. THE BORROW PROXY DOES NOT WORK. I built it to answer the one question history could not: can we borrow when it matters? 26h of data says the instrument is dead. BTC/ETH/USDT controls were EXACTLY flat (1.0x, zero variation) and LRC moved 1.1x while escalated. OKX publishes an ADMINISTERED tier rate, not a market-clearing one, so rate stability tells us nothing about pool availability. The question is still open and now provably unanswerable from public data — only an authenticated account attempting a real borrow settles it. || 2. DELISTING RISK, PREVIOUSLY UNMODELLED. LRC escalated 2026-07-17 and was DELISTED 2026-07-20 — three days later. Base rate: 1 of our 10 study episodes is already gone, within 3-17 days. This matters because a delisted perp cash-settles while the SHORT SPOT hedge leg survives: you are left naked short a borrowed coin in a name that just lost its derivatives market. Not modelled in the 48bps execution assumption, and not rare. || 3. SURVIVORSHIP BIAS IN THE ORIGINAL STUDY — the serious one. A delisted symbol loses its funding history entirely (LRC now returns 0 rows from funding-rate-history). Our 10 episodes were scanned across CURRENTLY LISTED symbols, so every coin that escalated and then died is INVISIBLE to that dataset. Direction of the bias: dying coins carry the most extreme funding and the worst outcomes, so we likely OVER-measured the profit and UNDER-measured the risk. The disasters delete themselves from the record. || 4. Pin duration is far shorter than episode duration. Live: LRC cap_bind->cap_release = 2.5h, LA pin_start->pin_end = 2.2h. The 26h median I reported was measured by INTERVAL, which stays short long after the cap stops binding. The extreme funding window is hours, not a day. || 5. Rate of occurrence confirmed low: 2 escalation coins in 4 days across 512 symbols, and one of the two (LA) is NOT BORROWABLE at all. || NEXT: the survivorship problem is fixable — snapshot funding history for escalating symbols AT THE TIME, before they can be delisted. The borrow question is not fixable from public data.

params.test: live tradeability, 5 days of borrow tracking

tests.note: LIVE TRADEABILITY IS THE PROBLEM, and 5 days of tracking now says so directly. Two new escalations since the last check: LA-USDT-SWAP (pin_start x2, then escalate 4h->2h + cap_bind) and ONE-USDT-SWAP (pin_start, escalate 8h->4h + cap_bind). Both are NOT BORROWABLE on OKX at all — 137 and 29 observations respectively, every one returning no borrow quota. SCORECARD OF EVERY COIN WE HAVE TRACKED LIVE: LRC borrowable but DELISTED three days later; LA not borrowable; ONE not borrowable. Three for three untradeable. Delta-neutral needs a SHORT SPOT leg, which needs borrow; no borrow means no hedge means no trade, regardless of how rich the funding looks. ACROSS ALL 12 ESCALATING COINS SEEN (live + historical): only 4 are borrowable = 33.3%, against an OKX baseline of 164/411 = 39.9%. I ALMOST OVERSTATED THIS AND AM RECORDING THE CORRECTION: the direction matches the hypothesis that funding stays pinned BECAUSE the coin cannot be borrowed, but binomial P(k<=4 | n=12, p=0.399) = 0.44. That is NOT significant. n=12 is far too small. The mechanism remains plausible and unproven. WHAT IS NOT IN DOUBT is the absolute number: only ~1 in 3 escalation episodes can be hedged at all, whatever the reason. Combined with the borrow quota ceiling ($1,834-$7,622 per coin) and the delisting risk already logged, the practical opportunity is far thinner than the +322bps median capture implied. BORROW PROXY CONFIRMED DEAD as an instrument: controls BTC/ETH/USDT show exactly 1.0x variation over 649 observations, and LRC moved 1.06x. OKX publishes an administered tier rate. It cannot reveal availability. STILL tested, not failed — the funding is real and the timing check still passes. But the path to trading it is now blocked in 2 of 3 observed cases, and that is the binding fact.

FIRST GO SIGNAL IN THE PROJECT. BARD-USDT-SWAP escalated 4h->2h with cap_bind, AND is borrowable, AND has a spot market — all three conditions met for the first time (LRC was borrowable but delisted; LA/ONE/O had no borrow, and O had no spot at all). Detection, GitHub-issue alert and episode recording all fired automatically with no human action. NOT A WIN — the money question is unresolved and resolves within 2 hours. THE TIMING PROBLEM SHOWED UP AGAIN, exactly as flagged: the 24h funding total is -1.0289%, of which -1.0000% is the SINGLE 12:00 settlement that hit the floor and TRIGGERED the escalation. That one is already paid — not capturable. Strip it and the trailing 24h is -0.0289%. BREAKEVEN: borrow costs 0.6301%/day; at a 2h interval that is 12 settlements/day, so we need >0.0525% per settlement just to cover borrow, before the ~46bps round-trip execution (perp spread 15.5bps + spot 7.7bps, both legs, in and out). THE FORK, and it is genuinely open: pre-escalation settlements ran 0.005-0.018% (BELOW breakeven -> napkin KILL, -87bps, -10%/yr). The current quote is 0.428% (8.2x ABOVE breakeven -> +405bps, +48%/yr) but it is method=current_period, i.e. an estimate that keeps moving until settlement, NOT locked. Napkin returns UNKNOWN on that scenario for exactly the right reason: R5b, edge is estimated not observed. CAPACITY IS THE OTHER PROBLEM, and it may bind harder than the rate: quota is $2,505 but spot top-5 depth is only $680. Even in the optimistic case this is ~$100/day on ~$2.5k, and only while the episode lasts (hours). ACTION: RECORD, do not trade. The recorder is already capturing BARD every 10 min including spot price — the first complete row we have ever had. The next 2-3 settlements settle the question at zero cost. The one thing recording still cannot answer: whether the borrow actually FILLS. That needs a real account attempting a tiny real borrow — the user, not me.

FIRST MEASURED PASS — the fork opened yesterday resolved in favour of the idea. All 8 post-escalation settlements cleared the 0.0525%/settlement breakeven, by 2.5x to 10x: -0.2089, -0.2457, -0.1659, -0.1279, -0.5350, -0.3310, -0.4023, -0.3904. This is OBSERVED, not the current_period estimate that made yesterday UNKNOWN. The pessimistic scenario (rates revert to the 0.005-0.018% pre-escalation range) did NOT happen over 14 hours. HONEST P&L, 14h episode: funding +2.4071%, borrow -0.3308% (0.5671%/day), execution -0.46% one-time (perp spread 15.5bps + spot 7.7bps, both legs, in and out) => NET +1.6163% on notional over 14h. Napkin with edge_measured=True: PASS, +161.63bps per round trip, +48.49%/yr annualised. BUT R6 FIRES AS A WARNING AND IT IS THE REAL STORY: the percentage is excellent and the absolute amount is tiny. Spot top-5 depth is $680, so a safe fill is ~$11 for the whole 14h episode; at the full $2,505 borrow quota it is ~$40. At a historical rate of ~1 hedgeable episode per 4 days that is roughly $330/yr on $680 deployed. Real, positive, risk-adjusted — and small. STILL NOT DEPLOYED, and the reason is unchanged: n=1 episode, and the one question no amount of recording can answer is whether the BORROW ACTUALLY FILLS when everyone wants it. That needs a real account attempting a tiny real borrow. WHAT THIS DOES SETTLE: the entry/exit rule is a CONDITION CHECK, not a prediction. Enter when escalated (interval <= 2h) AND borrowable AND settlements above breakeven; exit when the interval reverts or funding falls below breakeven. No forecast required at any point — which is exactly why this one is measurable and the directional signal (49.3% over 6,939 predictions) never was.

funding_escalation.notes: PRE-REGISTERED 2026-07-18 BEFORE measuring any funding collected. || DATA UNLOCK: I previously told the user this needed WEEKS of snapshotting because the interval flag has no history. That was WRONG. /api/v5/public/funding-rate-history returns ~100 settlements per symbol with fundingTime stamps, and the GAP between consecutive settlements IS the interval — so escalation history is reconstructable retroactively. Verified before registering: LRC-USDT-SWAP gaps {4h:81, 2h:1, 1h:17} (full 4->2->1 ladder), HOME-USDT-SWAP {4h:88, 2h:11}. ~15 days of history per symbol, 510 symbols. I have looked ONLY at interval gaps, NOT at rates collected. Bar set below is therefore blind. || PREDICTION: an escalation episode delivers materially more funding to the receiving side than a normal period, enough to clear a delta-neutral round trip. || PRE-SET BAR (fixed now, cannot move): total funding collected over one escalation episode must exceed 40 bps. Derivation: delta-neutral needs spot+perp on both entry and exit = 4 legs; OKX maker spot 0.08% + perp 0.02% = 20bps round trip OPTIMISTIC (maker on all four legs); R3 requires 2x => 40bps. If episodes deliver <=40bps, REJECT — same death as funding_carry, and no amount of escalation drama changes it. || MUST ALSO PASS: (a) NOTIONAL/PERSISTENCE — episodes must be long enough that the funding actually accrues, not a single settlement; (b) TIMING — the signal is only tradeable if we can enter AFTER escalation is observable and STILL collect; if the fat funding lands on the settlement that TRIGGERS escalation, we are too late by construction and it is unlearnable. This second check is the one most likely to kill it. || PRIOR: funding_carry already failed at ~0.8%/yr unleveraged. This is the same mechanism in an extreme state, so the honest question is whether the extreme state is big enough and lasts long enough. Expect the timing check to be decisive.
